aliases: Leavened Flatbread with Garlic and Cheese

english: Leavened Flatbread with Garlic and Cheese

region_of_origin: Indian Subcontinent (North India); served in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general naan bread class (FOODON_03302050); no specific garlic-and-cheese naan subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific naan or garlic-cheese naan code. A00LC (Bread, leavened) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 covers naan bread generically; no garlic-and-cheese naan specific code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of garlic and cheese naan weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served in a North Indian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Naan, plain, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 42234); adapted for garlic-and-cheese variant using ICMR-NIN cheese and garlic composition data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / ICMR-National Institute of Nutrition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Plain naan values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 42234: Bread, naan, plain, commercially prepared). Cheese contribution estimated using ICMR-NIN Table of Food Composition (2017) for processed cheese (~25 g per 100 g naan). Garlic contribution is minor and consistent with USDA garlic data. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate adjusted proportionally for a typical garlic-and-cheese naan formulation. This is an adapted estimate; exact restaurant formulation may vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; White wheat bread / naan analogue
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for garlic-and-cheese naan specifically. GI of 71 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for white wheat bread / plain naan (GI ~71, high category), as documented in Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. The addition of cheese and garlic does not substantially alter the GI relative to the plain naan base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (71 × 56.9) / 100 ≈ 40.4, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 47.5 × 1.2 = 57.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~576 mg per standard 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit garlic and cheese naan to occasional consumption, as a single serving contributes approximately 576 mg sodium — roughly 25% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — and should request reduced-salt or butter-free preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 71) and high glycemic load per serving (~40)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume garlic and cheese naan sparingly, as its high GI (71) and high glycemic load (~40 per serving) can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with high-fibre vegetables, lentils, or raita and limiting portion to half a piece may help moderate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese and butter (~4.2 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, garlic and cheese naan should be consumed in moderation due to its saturated fat content from cheese and ghee/butter, which may contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation; choosing a smaller portion and pairing with vegetable-based dishes is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Garlic and Cheese Naan is a popular North Indian leavened flatbread made from refined white wheat flour (maida), enriched with cheese (typically processed or mozzarella) and fresh or minced garlic, then baked in a tandoor (clay oven) or on a tawa (griddle). The dough is prepared with yogurt, milk, a leavening agent (yeast or baking powder), and salt, giving the bread a soft, slightly chewy interior and a lightly charred, blistered exterior. Garlic is incorporated into the dough or applied as a topping along with butter or ghee, and cheese is either stuffed inside or melted on top, producing a rich, savoury, aromatic flatbread. In Singapore, garlic and cheese naan is commonly served in North Indian restaurants as an accompaniment to curries, dals, and grilled meats, and is priced around SGD 7 per piece. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense (~310 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~9.5 g), significant carbohydrate (~47.5 g), and notable fat (~8.5 g) from cheese and butter. Its high glycemic index (GI ~71) and high glycemic load per serving (~40) make it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or blood glucose levels. The sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g) is also relevant for those with hypertension. The cheese contributes calcium (~120 mg per 100 g) and saturated fat (~4.2 g per 100 g), which is relevant for cardiovascular health. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in garlic cheese naan?
Is garlic naan good for diabetics?
What is the GI of naan bread?
How much sodium is in garlic cheese naan?
Can I eat naan if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between plain naan and cheese naan?

ingredient_synonyms.paneer: Indian cottage cheese (note: in this context cheese refers to processed or mozzarella, not paneer)
ingredient_synonyms.tandoor: clay oven used for baking naan

confidence_reason: No exact garlic-and-cheese naan entry was found in ICMR-NIN or HPB Singapore Foodo databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy plain naan data with proportional adjustment for cheese and garlic content based on ICMR-NIN cheese composition data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (plain white naan/white bread, GI 71) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this food; cheese may contribute trace amounts but no reliable per-100g figure is available from the approved sources used. 2. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary adapted source. 4. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Grains and staples', as garlic and cheese naan is primarily a leavened flatbread; cheese is a secondary ingredient. 5. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate based on plain naan / white wheat bread from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008). 6. Nutrient values represent a typical restaurant-style garlic and cheese naan; exact values will vary by restaurant formulation, cheese type, and butter quantity. 7. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy naan and cheese composition data and should be treated as approximate. 8. The input food_group 'Dairy and eggs' was not adopted as the primary classification; 'Grains and staples' is more accurate for a flatbread product.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from input value 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Grains and staples' as naan is primarily a flatbread.
Canonicalized food_name from 'Garlic N Cheese Naan' to 'Garlic and Cheese Naan'.
Added mandatory multilingual local_names: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check.
Sourced nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (plain naan) adapted with ICMR-NIN cheese data.
Assigned GI 71 (high) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Applied correct ontology codes with closest_parent match_type and gap notes for all three ontology systems.
